{"query":"Berkshire Hathaway Tokio Marine Holdings stake acquisition percentage cost 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1442,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.businessinsurance.com/berkshire-hathaway-to-take-2-5-stake-in-tokio-marine/","title":"Berkshire Hathaway to take 2.5% stake in Tokio Marine - Business Insurance","content":"Mar 23, 2026 — Berkshire Hathaway to take 2.5% stake in Tokio Marine ・ ly selling a 2.49% stake ・ proceeds of up to 287.4 billion yen ($1.80 billion)
Berkshire Hathaway in 2019 kicked off a number of investments in Japanese trading companies and the firm has increased its stakes multiple times since then.
The two firms will also execute joint investments globally, including mergers and acquisitions, Tokio Marine added.
Tokio Marine said it would use the proceeds of up to 287.4 billion yen ($1.80 billion) to buy back its own shares to prevent dilution for existing shareholders."},{"url":"https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/tokio-marine-to-form-strategic-partnership-with-berkshire-hathaway-initially-sell-249-stake-4574263","title":"Berkshire Hathaway takes $1.8 billion stake in Japan’s Tokio Marine, forms partnership By Reuters","content":"TOKYO, March 23 (Reuters) - Berkshire Hathaway, the conglomerate built by Warren Buffett, is buying a 2.49% stake in Japanese insurer Tokio Marine Holdings for about $1.8 billion as part of a new strategic partnership, deepening its financial commitment to Japan.
Tokio Marine said on Monday it will sell about 48.2 million treasury shares to National Indemnity, one of Berkshire’s main reinsurance businesses.
National Indemnity will assume part of Tokio Marine’s portfolio, and can boost its stake to 9.9% through open-market purchases without approval from Tokio Marine’s board.
Those stakes were worth $35.4 billion as of December 31, more than twice what Berkshire paid."},{"url":"https://sg.finance.yahoo.com/news/berkshire-hathaway-stake-us-1-000110976.html","title":"Berkshire Hathaway to stake US$1.8 bil in insurer Tokio Marine","content":"(March 23): Berkshire Hathaway Inc will invest ¥287.4 billion (US$1.8 billion or $2.3 billion) in insurer Tokio Marine Holdings Inc, marking a significant increase in the US conglomerate's exposure to the Japanese market.
National Indemnity Company, a subsidiary of Berkshire, will make a 2.49% strategic investment in Tokio Marine, and the two companies will collaborate on reinsurance and mergers and acquisition, according to a statement on Monday."},{"url":"https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/us/news/mergers-acquisitions/buffetts-berkshire-takes-1-8-billion-stake-in-japans-tokio-marine-569438.aspx","title":"Buffett's Berkshire takes $1.8 billion stake in Japan's Tokio Marine | Insurance Business","content":"Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway is deepening its bet on Japan, with subsidiary National Indemnity Company (NICO) set to acquire a 2.49% stake in Tokio Marine Holdings for ¥287.4 billion ($1.8 billion) as part of a sweeping strategic partnership between two of the world's largest insurance groups.
Under the deal, NICO will take the stake through a third-party allotment of treasury shares.
NICO has agreed not to acquire more than 9.9% of the Japanese insurer without prior board approval.
Tokio Marine, Japan's oldest insurance company, reported consolidated total assets of ¥31.2 trillion (roughly $205 billion) as of March 2025, with adjusted net income of approximately ¥1.07 trillion for the fiscal year.
NICO, widely regarded as one of the best-capitalized insurers in the world, carries an A++ rating from A.M.
Berkshire Hathaway's total insurance float stood at approximately $176 billion at the end of 2025.
Under the deal, Japan committed up to $550 billion in US investment over three years - a figure the St."}]}
